description: Stale comment at libs/mng_claude/imbue/mng_claude/plugin.py:1384 still references old terminology: 'source_path=None (clone/no-git) means trust is prompted for work_dir'. Should say '(rsync/none)' instead of '(clone/no-git)' to match the new transfer mode naming.

In libs/mng/imbue/mng/cli/create.py:1196-1198, is_rsync_enabled is set to True when transfer_mode is TransferMode.NONE. However, _create_work_dir_in_place (the NONE mode handler) never checks or uses is_rsync_enabled. Setting it to True for NONE mode is a no-op that could mislead readers.

Test docstring for test_provision_does_not_extend_trust_for_non_worktree claims '_find_git_source_path returns None' but for a regular git repo (not a worktree), it returns the work_dir itself (parent of .git). The end effect is the same (no meaningful trust extension from source to self) but the docstring is misleading.

The _create_work_dir_via_git_mirror method in host.py does not validate that options.git is not None when transfer_mode is GIT_MIRROR. If an API caller passes transfer_mode=GIT_MIRROR with git=None, the method creates an empty target directory (no transfer happens). The CLI validates this, but the host-level API does not.
The new spec files (specs/electron-desktop-app/ and specs/vps-docker-provider/) are unrelated to the consolidate-transfer-trois branch and appear to have been accidentally included.
